title: dioscorea nipponica subsp. rosthornii
intro: Dioscorea nipponica subsp. rosthornii, also known as Japanese wild yam, is a species of yam native to East Asia, specifically in China, Japan, and Korea. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits of Dioscorea nipponica subsp. rosthornii include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of menopause and osteoporosis. Dioscorea nipponica subsp. rosthornii can be prepared as an herbal remedy through various methods, such as decoction, infusion, and powder, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
